rag_rules: Place generated AGREE annex blocks in the target component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Preserve AADL architecture by not modifying existing component types or implementations
Use only visible identifiers in generated contracts when no explicit whitelist is available
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope for the contract
Preserve existing AADL structure when inserting annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers in generated contracts when no explicit whitelist is available
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence, for example `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Branching logic should use nested `if ... then ... else`
anti_patterns: Do not place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes without concrete bounds or visible relations
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use opaque Boolean data range comparisons unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
